title: El 8 de enero hice un pedido por valor…
text:
El 8 de enero hice un pedido por valor de 500€. Lo enviaron sin posibilidad de concretar franja horaria o recoger en un punto cercano. Lululemon ordenó el retorno del pedido a sus almacenes (lo sé por la empresa de mensajería). Lululemon no se ha puesto en contacto conmigo para comunicarme nada más que abrió una incidencia. Pero ni una palabra de la devolución del importe...
Es imposible entenderse, vía ticket, con su equipo de atención al cliente y el único teléfono que atiende venta online es del Reino Unido, olé!!!

title: Hice un pedido el 25 de diciembre
text: Hice un pedido el 25 de diciembre, me dijeron que llegaría el 5 de enero y nada, es super complicado ponerse en contactos con ellos, al ver el seguimiento solo dice en camino.

title: Estafa
text: He sufrido una estafa donde han utilizado mi tarjeta de debito para compras en esta tienda. Escribí a ellos en el mismo dia con la copia de la denuncia de la Policía de España solicitando que la transacción fuera cancelada y no han hecho caso. Por ser una tarjeta de débito no hay disputa del banco. Sofri esta estafa con otras compras y otros comercios si que han cancelado la transacción (comercios serios). Solo piensan en su dinero. El trato que me han dado con una persona que ha sufrido una estafa como la mía (ellos tenían la denuncia y podrán ver los detalles) ha sido el peor. Empresas así no deberían existir.

title: Desastroso como no he experimentado en mi vida…
text: Desastroso como no he experimentado en mi vida. No tratan al cliente con un mínimo de dignidad. Su combo con UPS es perverso. Sólo espero que me devuelvan el dinero ya que la mercancía se extravió. Estarán de moda pero no valen ni para escuchar su llueve.

title: Mejoren en el transporte
text: Mejoren en el transporte, UPS no funciona , todavía no  he recibido el pedido ni se cuando lo recibiré.

title: Excellent
text: Excellent. Lo recomiendo.

title: La transpirabilidad es demasiado buena
text: Compré un juego. Después de recibirlo, creo que el tejido es muy bueno y la transpirabilidad también es genial. Puedo usarlo para correr y hacer ejercicio. A mis amigos también les gusta. Ya lo he recomendado.

title: Dårlig kvalitet og dårlig kundeservice
text:
Købte i december en yogamåtte i deres webshop. Pris 690 dkk. Har ikke brugt den meget - alligevel kan jeg nu se at den begynder at gå fra i limningen. Jeg kontaktede deres customer support med kvittering, billeder etc. 
efter mange mails frem og tilbage hvor jeg gentagne gange er blevet bedt og at hensende billeder, og komme med de samme informationer flere gange - har jeg nu fået besked om at de ikke tager mod reklamationer som er mere end 12 mdr. Magen til dårlig kundeservice har jeg sjældent mødt. Køber aldrig noget der igen. Dårlig kvalitet og dårlig kundeservice men høje priser

title: I love Lululemon
text: I love Lululemon! I've never had a bad purchase, and their customer service is great.

title: The worst
text: The worst!   The shipper they use Ontrac have no communication with Lululemon and they are therefore completely unable to assist in an undelivered package.   You would think with the crazy expensive prices that users pay for their goods that they would have some basic abilities to assist customers.  I’m going to also complain to American Express Platinum card that uses this company as part of their benefits for cardholders.

title: Worst Customer Support ever
text: I had what was probably the worst online shopping experience I've had in a long time. The zipper on a jacket broke after four weeks. I contacted Lululemon and received what was probably the most dismal online complaint handling service ever. I've had to submit my complaint five times now, providing the same information each time, because I keep getting responses from different employees. I receive standard emails that make no sense, and the online return forms don't work. Never again.

title: this is my first order from lulu so i…
text:
update: they got a hold of me and im getting a replacement. let you know if this pans out for me.

this is my first order from lulu so i was excited. but they courier they use deilivered it outside of my condo complex, ofcourse it will get stolen. i havent seen someone delivery it outside. so i contact them, it takes 24 hours to file and 2-3 busienss day to resolve the issue. i need the times in 1 week for a photoshoot. terrible experience for my frist time on this lulu.

title: Un service plus que déplorable
text:
Un service plus que déplorable. Une première commande effectuée début Janvier, jamais arrivée, et à ce jour, j’en suis à ma 4 ième commande non livrée et remboursée sans compensation, avec un service client déplorable et une politique de satisfaction client inexistante.

Impressionné de voir une marque de ce standing avoir un service aussi peu professionnel. 

Passez votre chemin, ou prenez le risque de vous battre pour récupérer (beaucoup) d’argent dépensé dans le vent!

title: Bad customer service.
text: Bad customer service.  Tried to find a product on line and did not see it so I called customer service to see if it was discontinued. She could not answer the question and said the design team likes to surprise us with what they will have.  I said that's bad customer service and it's idiotic because customers want to know if a specific product they like is available or discontinued. She hung up on me.  Because I said not letting customers know if specific products are discontinued is idiotic and bad customer service.

title: Undelivered items
text: Bought items last month which were never delivered. DPD claims it was delivered to a parcel shop at 18:30 on a Friday. Parcel shop agent said the item is not there and that the shop closes at 18:00, so it could not have delivered. Numerous back and forth between DPD and Lululemon chat where both say the parcel was delivered and NOTHING IS DONE. This is driving me insane, as I cannot retrieve the parcel or the money paid!

title: Awful customer service
text: Bought a pair of the abc pants a year ago and they are coming undone at the seams. I messaged customer service and they said they can’t do anything about it because it’s over their year warranty by just a few days. Horrible customer service, you make so much money off your customers and you can’t even help them just once. I’ve never had to return anything to lululemon till now. Will not be purchasing from them again.

title: Very poor quality leggings
text: Very poor quality leggings

title: My package delivered to the wrong…
text:
My package delivered to the wrong address, back and forth first with virtual messages from delivery company,
then with a real person, back and forth, finally they could not recover the package. Used live chat with Lululemon, did not get any where , called them after reading messages, I have been told I should wait 24 hours and then call again to reorder it, what a customer friendly service.

title: Lacking in store stock for the most part
text: Wish they had better availability of sizes in store. It's hard to differenciate the fabrics online, new collections launched too frequently

title: Lululemon terrible customer service expensive also
text: Lululemon said a delivery has arrived I went look for it all over.  I texted them because you cannot talk to anyone live in customer service.  The said it was delivered.  After going back and forth with them.  They sent me photo were is was delivered.  It wasn't even on my property. There is a cemetary next to my property and they just flung the 2 white package into snow next to my property.  Did not even come down my street. I will never order anything again from this incompetent company.  Overpriced  and terrible customer service.  If I could give ZERO STARS I would.

title: Consumer Data and Privacy Issues
text:
I ordered a pair of pants online and stopped by a Lululemon store to try them on. When they didn't work, I went to the register to return them and was told I couldn't return them until the next day because the purchase "hadn't run through their system yet." This was confusing, considering my credit card had already been charged.

What made the situation worse was what came next: I was told that in order to process the return, the store would need to scan my California ID -- not just look at it, but scan it into their system.

This is deeply concerning. In California, scanning a government ID is not legal unless the practice is clearly disclosed and the purpose is explicitly stated, typically for fraud prevention in limited situations. That was not the case here. No disclosure, no explanation, just "this is the only way we can do the return."

There is also no logical reason for this request. My ID is not tied to my online purchase, it is not "in their system," and there is no matching function taking place. If the transaction truly hadn't posted yet, scanning my ID would not solve that issue -- so why collect that data at all?

Between the system excuse that doesn't align with the credit card charge and the insistence on scanning my ID without transparency, this experience felt unnecessary, invasive, and poorly handled. I expect better from a brand that positions itself as premium and customer-centric.

I left without completing the return and with serious concerns about Lululemon's in-store practices around customer data and privacy.

title: Sketchy return policy
text: Bought two pairs of shoes on sale, returned one of them by post with no problem then tried to return the second pair still within the 30 days at a store I was at. They told me it was marked final sale and that I could only get in store credit. I had my order slip that did not show final sale plus I was allowed with no problem to return the exact same shoe already by post. Talk to two employees, they both told me there was nothing they could do. I looked at their site and the shoe is now on final clearance but it was not when I bought it. I feel this is dishonest and I will never shop here again.

title: First time customer who purchased items…
text:
First time customer who purchased items to use the new Amex platinum benefit. They only put one piece of tape on the box to seal it and had 1” hanging over the sides.  Unsurprisingly to no one, this left the box to open mid transit.  Half the items didn’t show, the other half were dirty.  The way to contact them forced me to use their AI “live help chat” which just routes you to a whole seperate place to have to enter a 800 digit order # by hand OR abandon the page to go and copy it from the email you clicked to get into the help page to begin with (which was the way I went about it after trying and failing to find an easy route for order help on their webpage).  

It took 15 min for a process that shouldn’t take more than 2-3 bc they are too cheap to hire customer service.  And now, all I am told is my claim has been received.  Zero apologies.  Just a we got your claim after you took the 78 steps we forced you to go through to do so.  And now, we will get back to you with what we decide to do about it - without any input from the customer or personal support of any kind.  Not having this resolved from one single quick touch base from your customer is absolutely unacceptable for any product from a self proclaimed luxury provider.  I have a better experience when I buy yoga pants from old navy - consistently.  That should embarrass the heck outta you. 

Your stuff is CRAZY overpriced.  And this is what you get for those price pts with undoubtedly 95% mark ups?  That’s shameful.  Platinum benefit or not I won’t be using it again if this is the labor you put on your customer base for a baseball hat and some socks.

title: Team Canada cross body bag cheap quality!
text: I ordered the team Canada Future Legacy Easy Access 1.5 litre bag and was disappointed when it arrived. It’s cheap plastic, looks like something from the dollar store. The Olympic patch on the front is also cheap looking. The bag is smaller than I had anticipated, not like my other Lululemon 1.5 Litre crossbody bag. My phone barely fits in this. Don’t waste your money.

title: Don’t trust their repair guarantee!
text:
I’m extremely disappointed with lululemon’s so called “quality” and repair promise.

After my coat tore during normal wear on a simple country walk right next to a seam I contacted customer service expecting support or a repair especially given the brand’s reputation and premium pricing.

What followed was weeks of back and forth repeated forms photo submissions and delays only to be told in the end that nothing could be done. No repair. No goodwill. No solution.

Despite being a loyal customer for years I was treated like a case number rather than a valued customer. Their “quality promise” sounds great in theory but in my experience it is not worth the paper it is written on.

If you are buying from lululemon expecting long lasting products and support when something goes wrong think carefully. Once they have your money you may be on your own.

I will not be shopping with them again.

title: Eindeloos wacht ik nu al op mijn…
text: Eindeloos wacht ik nu al op mijn bestelling. Meerder mails gestuurd met de vraag waar mijn order blijft. Uiteindelijk een bericht met dat je maar moet wachten. Ik zou hier nooit een bestelling plaatsen. Gelukkig met Klarna betaald dus hoop dat ik mijn geld niet kwijt ben!

title: The Olympic outfits are absolutely…
text:
The Olympic outfits are absolutely appallingly. They are drab and make are athletes look terrible. Who did this did you not know your garments would be on display in one of the world’s fashion capitals.
Chip Wilson was right nobody knows what they are doing.
I sure hope you are not doing the next one.

title: Lululemon material quality has gone Down big time!
text: I bought a pair of yoga pants 3 months ago from this shop, paid €98. I wore them a handful of times, washed them 30c with dark colors and always hung dried them. I live in Italy, and don’t have a dryer. I had baught from lululemon in the past and had no problems. However, this time, the quality of the product and material have really gone down. I have tiny little balls on my pants in between the legs and on the top of the right thigh. I wrote customer service, and they said that I do my washing wrong, that I should wash only in cold water and that basically I am the problem, not their product. Soon they will sell yoga pants, but you should not practice yoga in them, you should only stare at them, and not too hard or they will get ruined. I will never again spend money on this brand. The quality is the same as a pair of yoga pants that costs 30€. I will stick to the cheaper ones, that feel the same and have the same lifespan. Pants  produced in Cambodia…produced at a very low cost and sold overpriced. they might want to quality control better and revise their customer service. Never again!!

title: Trash clothes for hundreds made from…
text: Trash clothes for hundreds made from 100 percent polyester that don’t even last 6 months

title: 3rd time they tried to take the cheaper…
text: 3rd time they tried to take the cheaper route with delivery and my items never made it to me !!!!! The costumer’s service is terrible and they don’t even care they the company is being ripped off by clowns

title: Holy hole btwn legs!
text:
I used to buy so many fine quality lululemon products but have stopped purchasing due to their horribly cheaper quality and terrible seams.  I received a gift a while back (align leggings) and the seam has split right near the "undercarriage" in one spot....its evident that whoever did the sewing rushed and didnt sew over enough of the material. It is perfectly split..
I have had pants that last years without any issues and the more expensive brand cant even sew a seam shut. Horrible and do not reccommend until they bring their previous quality material back.

title: Defective product which I returned and…
text: Defective product which I returned and they refused refund even though I gave them proof of delivery. Bad actors

title: Gift Cards Should Be More User-Friendly!
text: Got a gift card recently for a refund when I returned some sale items. They had a computer glitch at the store, so I had to get a physical card. Now the system doesn’t recognize the gift card. So I will have to make another trip to the store. What a waste of my time! Just refund to our credit card for sale items, and if you can’t do that, then make sure the amount goes automatically into our account. I am convinced that this practice of refusing to refund to the same method of payment is just a company ploy to make more money - they are counting on people losing or forgetting about their gift cards. Or simply getting so frustrated that they give up. DO BETTER LULU!

title: Missing Items, no support from Customer Service
text:
I ordered 2 items for a christmas gift. I received the package but only one item was in the package. I contacted them and they first said too bad because it was delivered to an office and not a home?  Does it make a difference? The package was not tampered with and had nothing to do with where the item was shipped. After contacting them mulitiple times, they couldn't understand that it was their packing error - they would not read the email. Then they came back and said " oh we understand you received someone else's package" again, not what i said. I finally sent them picture of the packing slip with a big note says "ITEM NOT RECEIVED". Now after  3 follow up emails, they are completely ignoring me.  
What I do find odd is they originally said to me "call you bank to dispute the charge' I can't believe that credit card companies would even allow them to say that. Regardless, lululemon customer service is by far the worst I have ever encountered.

title: I recently bought online Define Nulu…
text: I recently bought online Define Nulu jacket in new Porcelain Pink color. It’s doesn’t fit me, right shoulder is overstretched, stitches are not straight, material is thin and cheap. Have the same model in navy blue- the quality of Pink one dropped unacceptably . Waisted $128 ‼️.

title: No consideration for circumstances
text:
Lululemon customer service return policy is ridiculous! I have 2 align skirts purchased spring brand new with tags and rip tags that I purchased and they were two small so I purchased two more with the same store not even a week later! I had taken the two that were too small in for a refund, but they will only give store credit even tho they could put them right back on the shelf for sale!
I am a single mom who needs the $200 right now and they will only offer me a gift card! I don’t need more clothing I need to pay my bills so this is absolutely off putting and I will never shop with them again!

title: Dishonest, manipulating, and lacking integrity
text: I made a purchase online and was sent used/previously returned items. This was a final sale order so I couldn't return the items despite being grossed out that I didn't receive sealed items. I left a poor review on the Lululemon site and they replied saying I would be reached out to. I was emailed by their customer care who listed me a few options I could choose from and to respond back. I responded back with my selection and was completely ghosted/ignored from that point on. I have not heard back from them at all. It was all a manipulation tactic, to appear like they care and have integrity within the review forum, but in reality and behind the scenes, they do not. When I went to update my review on the Lululemon site, I found they obviously didn't publish it, to further hide their true colours. Lululemon is a despicable company.

title: Good customer service, ok quality
text: I've been shopping at Lulu since forever and I still love them but their quality has definitely gone down. I'm giving them 4 stars though because they still honour their policy of trading in items of poor quality (like pilling) as long as it's within 1 year of purchase. I appreciate the customer service.

title: Returning something is almost impossible
text: Received Lululemon clothes as a gift but everything was huge. When I went to return them, the website said the return window had passed, but it actually didn't. So I had to contact their customer service. They processed the returns and said they sent a QR code to my email which they said I could take to FedEx. It took 2 days for them to get me the labels and I still had to go back to them to get the 3rd they forgot. I said the labels they gave me were for USPS but they insisted they could go to FedEx. 3 items each needed their own shipping box for each of the 3 return labels so it was hell trying to match up the order numbers with the items because the person bought me 3 identical looking things that were actually all different styles somehow. When I got to fedex they told me I was given a USPS label. I would never recommend this company. EVER. Their return system is so chaotic and confusing and you're getting misinformation from representatives who are not even in this country and are not familiar with US shipping companies.

title: Worst exchange policies in the industry
text: Lululemon used to be the pinnacle in fitness clothing due to their amazing service, but they have fallen off a cliff. Their policies are now appalling. I got given clothes for my birthday that didn't quite fit. When I wanted to exchange them they said no because they were marked down. What a horrible policy that doesn't provide any option to exchange clothes within a reasonable timeframe. I'll never shop at Lululemon again.

title: Rude online shop assistants
text:
I am a very loyal Lululemon customer, but am very disappointed after speaking to an online shop advisor - Hannah today.
I was chasing a refund which appears to have been missed by them and was spoken to so rudely. The irony is that they issue a lengthy message which is given when you call to ask for a polite interaction, which clearly does not apply to their employees. Very disappointing when they are a company based on health and wellness. Not surprising they are losing customers to Alo and Vuori.

title: Lululemon is not the hype
text: I've been buying Lululemon for a couple of years. Even around that time the quality was not like earlier years. They basically became a quick fashion type deal. Its understandable when you have to switch to a more affordable fabric but for them to raise prices on clothing that doesn't justify the price is mad. I purchased a pair of lululemon Align™ High-Rise Mini-Flare Pant *Extra Short December 2024. They were on sale from 118 to 80 dollars. I wore them like 5 times and the thread started unraveling in 2 places the waist and leg hem. Then after like 2nd wear they started piling between the legs. I reached out a month pass a year and they told me it was wear and tear. What a joke of a company! I let them know the leggings that originally costed 118 dollars should not be falling apart after wearing them 5 times. Before this I purchased 2 items both terribly thin. Im glad I buy their crap on sale because I would NEVER spend full price at Lululemon. But now I will be looking elsewhere. This company is selling fast fashion subpar merchandise for quality prices. What a joke of a company.

title: I would leave zero stars if I could …
text: I would leave zero stars if I could as I ordered items on December 26th and I fully understand it was boxing day and I still have not received my order and it's January 17th !!! In fact they have not even updated anything since December 30th ! This is unacceptable for such a large corporation such as Lululemon. I am extremely disappointed with this level of service ! Not to mention I keep getting directed to several departments to resolve the issue. I will NEVER buy from this company again !!! Terrible service ! I still have not received my order after 23 days ? Lululemon get it together ! What a disgrace 😤

title: Horrible Customer Service and Poor Quality
text: I've been buying from Lululemon since 2014 and purchased multiple workout leggings over the years as well as other gear. I've never once had a quality issue or any other issue in this time. November 2025 I ordered a pair of "final sale" leggings that I actually already owned, just wanted an additional pair. Upon removing the tags and wearing them I find that the inseam of them is not the same as my prior pair or what was stated on the product description; and additionally the right leg is quite noticeably shorter than the left, about 1.5 inch difference. I reached out and they had me complete a "quality promise" form. It took nearly a week for them to respond and they stated it was not a quality issue but a fit preference and nothing would be done. I then called customer service and they told me they could not help me in any capacity, nor could they transfer me to a manager. I completed an additional quality form providing more pictures of the length discrepancy on the leggings and another week went by for them to say again, nothing was wrong and nothing would be done. I'm not even asking for a refund, just the correct product to be sent or even a store credit but they have zero desire to make it right. That speaks volumes about a company to me. I had recently read headlines regarding their fall in quality and customer service and have now unfortunately experienced it first hand. This ranks as one of, if not the most, disappointing customer experiences I've ever had with a company.

title: Lululemon customer service is horrible - not responsible
text: My package was shipped but never arrived. I waiting 2 weeks and checked. It is marked as lost in transit/undeliverable, but Lululemon did nothing to send a new one. When I tried to contact them I only got a robot. Robot collected my information and claimed to connect me to an agent, but agent never came on line. It's ridiculous.

title: Parfait
text: Super j’ai reçu ma commande dans les temps malgré une commande durant la période de noel

title: Disappointed
text: Disappointed, not nice service can’t come here anymore

title: I am so disappointed in this company
text: I am so disappointed in this company.  I buy their products all the time.  I wont be ordering on line again.  They sent me a wrong item and forgot two items I did order. The process to hopefully get the items I ordered took 25 minutes! And now they want me to return the card wallet they mistakenly sent me, which will also take time.   I own my own company and certainly would have compensated the customer if I had made an error.

title: Broken promises and Censorship.
text: Was promised delivery before Christmas. Delivered (without signature or photo) while I was away. Tried to leave one star review on their site but was censored.  What a useless 1990’s style of customer service.

title: Warte seit Wochen auf meine Erstattung
text:
Ich habe die Annahme eines Pakets verweigert, das laut Sendungsverfolgung bereits am 24.12. wieder bei lululemon eingegangen ist. Heute ist der 10.01. und ich habe mein Geld immer noch nicht zurückerhalten.

Der Kundenservice hat mir den Erhalt des Pakets zwar bestätigt, seitdem passiert jedoch nichts. Auf meine mehrfachen Nachfragen habe ich zweimal exakt die gleiche standardisierte Antwort erhalten.

Sehr enttäuschend ist vor allem die fehlende Transparenz und der Eindruck, dass man als Kunde nicht ernst genommen wird. So verliert man leider Vertrauen – und Kunden. Order Nummer #DE01180888

title: Good quality especially durability
text: Good quality especially durability

title: LILY AT ROOSEVELT FIELD LOCATON IS FANTASTIC!I
text: As I rarely write reviews, in this instance, you have one employee, working in your Roosevelt field Long Island Store, by the name of Lily, and as my Wife and I have been Loyal customers of Lululemon, Lily is one of the most phenomenal representations of a retail store, that we can remember. She is kind, articulate, thorough, thoughtful, and you can tell right through the phone, that she was excited to help with my questions regarding our account. The store and quite frankly, the company is absolutely Lucky to have her. I am Hoping that there will be some substantial benefit and gain for her, as she so deserves it. BTW – the call was not for anything to our benefit, and I am not lying this email because we receive something, but she represents Lululemon above and beyond what we have virtually every experienced. Please let us know that she will be properly acknowledged and rewarded in some capacity. Fondly Jay and Ruth

title: I’ve always had great experiences…
text: I’ve always had great experiences ordering from lulu lemon. Free shipping and my orders are always right. Customer service has always been great to me. I own a lot of their clothes and they last a long time. Never lose their shape or colour. I also take care of my clothes. Plus I’m Canadian I pay a lot because my country sucks.

title: Terrible Return Experience!!
text: I try to avoid the Jacksonville, Town Center store because it’s not a pleasant experience. Ever. But I had a few returns of a full price items. Had all my receipts. The guy pulled the items out of the lulu bag and one of the rip tags fell off. Price tag still in tact but that was it. He declined the return. I explained it was in tact and it was clear the shirt was brand new with price tag in tact. The size was just too big. He basically told me too bad, move on. So I’m stuck with a full price, brand new item that I cannot return??? Nice policy. I’ll be taking my hundreds, actually thousands at this point,  elsewhere!

title: Really comfortable and good quality
text: I shop there pretty often and the clothes are super comfortable. You can tell the quality right away when you wear them. The leggings are not too tight and they stay in place during workouts(common issue in some other brands). It's a bit pricey, so I don't buy from them that often.

title: Hice un pedido el 25 de diciembre
text: Hice un pedido el 25 de diciembre, me dijeron que llegaría el 5 de enero y nada, es super complicado ponerse en contactos con ellos, al ver el seguimiento solo dice en camino.

title: Quality Took a Nosedive
text: Recently bought a new pair of Aligns. Also recently returned from Vietnam where they’re made and sold from countless “factory outlet” stores in Hanoi and elsewhere. When I was there, I would pick up one of the Lulu knockoffs and the fabric felt like absolute garbage — I thought, that’s obviously not real Lulu. Lo and behold, I touch my brand new pair of ‘buttery soft’ Aligns, and it feels exactly like the ‘knockoffs’ I literally recoiled from in Vietnam. I bought a pair of Aligns in 2020 and they were actually soft, warm, almost like jersey, and I never worried about thong lines. The quality of this new pair is shamefully bad. Immediate return.

title: Lululemon devrait revoir leur service…
text: Lululemon devrait revoir leur service de livraison. Ils disent 2à10 jours ouvrables. Et chaque jours la dates de livraison change. Aujourd’hui fait 10 jours que j’attend et toujours rien reçu. title: Great Gear for South Florida, but Medium-Large Sizes Can Be Tough to Find
text:
I’ve been wearing Lululemon for years now, and it’s hands down some of the best activewear out there—especially in South Florida’s brutal heat and humidity. The fabrics are breathable and lightweight, making it easy to stay comfortable even during the hottest days. Whether I’m hitting the gym, running outside, or just doing everyday things, Lululemon keeps me cool and looking good.

The quality is second to none. Everything holds up over time, even with frequent wear, and the fit is always on point. Plus, I’ve had nothing but positive experiences with customer service—whether I’m in-store or shopping online, they’ve always been helpful and professional.

My only issue is with the availability of medium and large sizes. Some of the popular pieces are often sold out or hard to find in my size, which can be a little frustrating. Still, Lululemon’s quality and comfort make it worth the hunt. I’ll definitely keep coming back for more.

If you’re looking for activewear that performs well in the heat and lasts, Lululemon is the way to go!

title: Beyond disappointing
text:
I placed my very first order with Lululemon last week. At the checkout there was no option of choosing the shipping provider and the order was being shipped with Royal Mail. The ETA I received was 30.12.25, however it was delivered on 29.12.25 without any prior notifications of ‘out for delivery’ or ‘due to be delivered today’. At the time if the delivery I wasn’t in and assumed it was posted through my letterbox but once I got home my order was nowhere to be found. I checked the tracking number and ‘proof of delivery’ and it turns out that my order was left at a ‘safe place’ (outbuilding) that I didn’t nominate or game my consent to. Now I have neither my order nor my money! This is very stressful and very disappointing as the items at Lululemon are quite expensive. I’m very doubtful I will place another order with them.

Order number #UK04211316

title: I’ll start by stating that I’ve been a…
text: I’ll start by stating that I’ve been a Lulu fan for a long time but recently things have changed and been very disappointed primarily in their service. I’ve made several purchases this year and on 3 separate occasions I’ve made my order and then 3-5 days later (once even after it said it was shipped) they inform me that my order is cancelled due to inventory (most likely). They never used to do this and it’s a very bad practice. I’ve never had this happen with any other product I’ve ordered except Lulu. It’s like you’re trying to maximize sales at the expense of poor customer service. Either change the way you handle your orders and fix how you handle your inventory with online sales. I don’t like wasting my time and unless this is resolved you’ve lost a long time customer. I’m sure I’m not the only one that agrees with this poor practice. Get your service together and in order.

title: Degragating Quality
text:
I have been a long-term customer of lululemon since around 2014. Back then, I was consistently satisfied with the products I received. Although they were more expensive, the quality and fit were phenomenal. 

However, I was let down multiple times by lululemon in the past 2 years. 

1. The Textured Double-Knit Cotton Hoodie cost 128 CAD in 2021 and has increased to 158 CAD, now made with more plastic-like material.

2. The ABC pants changed their fit, and what was once the perfect fit for my athletic build is now baggy and fits more like pajamas.

3. The delivery is handled by a local network. Orders placed before 7:30am EST cannot be canceled because the only cancellation method is through the call center, which opens at 9:30 EST. I also submitted a change-of-delivery request, but Lululemon was unresponsive and did not confirm the address in time.

4. The Johnny Collar Polo (I can't recall the exact name) has an opening so tight that I have to literally squeeze my head into it.

5. They forgot to remove the security tag for me twice at two different physical stores, and the funny thing is I didn't even trigger the security alarm.


I'm pleased to see other companies catching up and gaining market share from lululemon, and I believe this trend is completely justified from my perspective. I have no issue paying more as long as I receive equivalent value in return :)

title: Non delivery of eGift voucher
text:

However, my niece has not received any voucher, and she has checked the spam folder.  I have tried to send emails.  This is the first time I have used their egift voucher service.  I would like a response to this query. 


The voucher finally arrived today 4 days later. I’ve never had this sort of experience when sending an e-voucher. I doubt I will use this service again given that this was meant to have arrived by Christmas Day.

title: Worthless Guarantee.
text:
The only reason I ordered from them was because THEY guranteed delivery by 12/24/25 if ordered before noon pst on 12/22/25. Yet on the morning of 12/23/25 my order was still processing. Support said just to wait for it to update. It updated and stated it would be delivered on time. 

However,  now that it is 12/24/25 I am told it won't be delivered until the 26th. Lululemon claims it is "Due to unforeseen circumstances with the shipping carrier. " 

They are full of it. This was fully foreseeable by me and false advertising on their end. Their stuff is good but when they drop the ball so bad on guaranteed delivery, and do not seem to be willing to take THEIR responsibility for it now being late, I will simply go elsewhere and talk smack about them from here on out.

title: Horrible clothes - even worst customer service
text: On 8 December 2025, I purchased 3 items from this company.  On 18 Dec 25, seeing nothing had shipped, I contacted the seller.  I was told that they made a mistake and would expedite my order with 2 day shipping.  They sent me a gift card that I then had to give back to them.  They also promised a $30 gift card for my trouble.  Fast forward to 22 Dec 25, and I still have not received what they promised, in writing.  To add insult to this, the first representative disconnected me and the second one claimed (1) she could find no order, (2) that as an appeasement they refunded the $78 to me, and that (3) they sent me to $30 gift card.  After I sent screen shots of the order, the representative was caught in her lies and admitted that there was an order - and part had shipped and another part would arrive in 2-3 days - AFTER Christmas which is why I ordered them on 8 December so they could arrive for Christmas.  I have asked for a $50 gift card for their egregious behavior and lies and was told to wait even longer for a reply.  This company is clearly engaged in deceptive behavior!

title: Awful customer service re: gift card
text:
My son went to use his gift card and was told the balance was $0.00. I contacted their resolution department and asked repeatedly to speak to a manager, only to be told that the person I was speaking with was “the highest person available.” "the decision is final" is what she said and "there are no exceptions".  At no time did I ask for an exception to be made.

She gave me the same scripted response over and over. They were able to tell me when the card was purchased and when it was used, but apparently not where it was used, which is exactly the information I needed to verify that the transaction wasn’t done by my son.

What makes this even more frustrating is that the card was given to him the very same day as the alleged use. That alone should raise red flags. Suspect. They refuse to provide the basic information needed to investigate it properly is unacceptable. SMH.

If you are thinking about buying gift cards for Christmas , reconsider, this is the 2nd time it has happened to me with receipts in hand.

title: I’m here to leave a bad review like…
text:
I’m here to leave a bad review like many others. I bought a $100 gift card, and when I first checked the balance, it showed the full $100. A few hours later, I checked again, and it said the card could not be found. It’s now being “looked at.”

I called customer service right away. The first representative I spoke to was very nice and helpful. She said she could see that the $100 was still on my card and wasn’t sure why it wasn’t showing up on my end.

Today, I called back to follow up, and I got no answers from the representative. She basically told me I was going to be out of luck — and then she hung up on me mid-sentence. Customer service literally hung up on me. It was absolutely horrendous.

Lululemon seems to be operating an organized scam with their gift cards. There are thousands of people who have been stolen from. Go check Reddit — it’s full of stories about Lululemon gift card fraud and issues with their security team. I don’t understand how they’re getting away with this. They are stealing their customers’ money left and right, all while providing the worst customer service imaginable.

My money was still showing on my card earlier, so I guess that’s one positive. I’m hoping to at least get a new card issued. I do not want them stealing my $100, and I will not let this go. You will hear from me every single day until I get a new card or my existing card is fixed — I promise you that.

I also want to point out that I did not have an account, so my card wasn’t attached to anything. According to what I’ve read on Reddit, attaching your card to your account is when it gets stolen most often. This is your warning: do not buy Lululemon gift cards unless you want your money stolen.

How does my card show $100 one moment, and then suddenly say it cannot be found? Because the money was taken. If I had read the reviews first, I would have never purchased this gift card. It was meant to be a gift, and now I can’t even give it.

I’m shocked that a large company like this is getting away with what is basically organized theft — a pattern of corrupt activity that needs to be fully investigated by the FBI or whoever handles this kind of consumer fraud. They have stolen thousands of dollars from customers and then hang up on people who simply want answers. It’s like they don’t care at all because they’ve gotten away with it for so long.

Karma will come.

Please issue me a new card or fix my existing one immediately. Otherwise, you will be hearing from me every single day until I get my money back. I promise you, you will get sick of hearing from me — so let’s handle this ASAP.

title: Experience with Carey Enzsweiler at Lululemon Ross Park Mall
text: I went to the Lululemon store at Ross Park to purchase outfits for a couple of women having surgery.  I hoped to find outfits for them to feel special at such a difficult time.  I have done this in the past and sometimes it works perfectly and other times not so perfect so I know to not purchase sale items.  I found two outfits - the one worked great and the other outfit the woman said did not fit.  I returned to the store less than 24 hours later with tags on - to return pants, jacket and a hat.  None of which was listed on sale.   The person at the desk (male with a beard) insisted that I had to have store credit.  I asked for a manager and he said that she was not in.  He gave me the in store gift card and I left with the store managers name.  I reached out to Carey Enzsweiler and the conversations have been unprofessional.  Ms. Enzsweilers response has been to tell me that she cannot find any proof that the tags were marked - they were not - and continues to say that she has to find more proof that I am telling the truth.  If there is not any proof that the tags were marked, why is this an issue?

title: Customer service is rude and unhelpful
text: Customer service is rude and unhelpful. Their products are overpriced, and some look very worn after a short period of normal use. Overall, Lululemon feels more focused on fashion than quality, and the poor customer service made it a really disappointing shopping experience.

title: Customer service
text:
From Italy, I purchased a €250 gift card online as a present for my daughter, who is their customer and lives in London. When she went to the store, she was told she couldn’t use it because it had been purchased on the European website (something that was not specified at the time of purchase). They did tell her, however, that contacting customer service would surely lead to a solution.

I reached out to them confidently, convinced that common sense would prevail and they would find a way to help me. Instead, they replied—with countless overly polite and irritating preambles—that my daughter will only be able to use the gift card when she travels around Europe or possibly in Milan, where their only Italian store is located. Alternatively, she could use it online on the European website, but she would have to pay shipping fees to receive the items in London.

I tried writing again to explain how impractical this solution was, but I never received another reply. They took the money, but I cannot use the gift card. Very unprofessional.

title: Horrible and outrageous service at Lululemon
text: I have never encountered such poor service!! In my life!!!!!!!! I ordered online for the first time on an order site for $334 including shipping. I knew I would have to pay taxes when it arrived, I just didn't know what I would have to go through. I waited a long time and then after 3 weeks of waiting, the FedEx courier company contacted me claiming that I had to pay taxes on a package worth $510!!! In other words, Lululemon made a mistake with the invoice value and now I have to chase them to change the invoice and send it to FedEx so that I can pay the correct taxes that correspond to the value of my order! I contacted them by email, Facebook and Instagram - an annoying bot answered me and I didn't get a response!!!!!! My package is lying at the airport without being handled and the company doesn't care at all!!! I order a lot online from Skims and ALO Yoga and I have never encountered such horrible and outrageous service in my life!!!!! First and last time I order from them! I hope you can help me solve the problem

title: A business with kinks on role playing educators
text:
The representative spoke to me in an arrogant and condescending manner, presenting themselves as an “educator.” They claimed that the store was doing me a favor by applying certain discounts and even threatened to revoke a discount I received as a gift, one that other customers have also used in the same way.  

When I brought this issue to the store manager’s attention, instead of addressing my negative experience, she chose to deflect my complaint and escalated the matter to the head office, addressing all account holders rather than resolving my specific concern.  

What made the experience worse was that both the staff and the manager seemed to believe their role was to “educate” customers. They even asked me to pass their so-called “education” on to senior members of my family regarding how to use their products, as the gift was from them; this was an entirely inappropriate and disrespectful request.  

Customers visit stores for positive experiences and to engage with products that suit their style, not to be subjected to unsolicited lectures or “educator” role-plays from brand representatives.

title: Lululemon Customer Experience Review
text:
Lululemon Customer Experience Review – Shockingly Poor for a Supposed Premium Brand

I recently purchased a pair of Lululemon pants, and upon receiving them, I immediately discovered the snaps were faulty. These are not inexpensive pants—they are marketed and priced as premium apparel—so I expected premium service in return.

Instead, the customer service agent refused to resolve the issue directly and immediately. Rather than offering an exchange or refund on the spot, I was directed to a website where I had to take photos, fill out forms, and essentially jump through hoops to prove the defect on a product I had just received. After doing all of that, I received an automated email informing me that someone would get back to me in 7–10 days—not to resolve it, but merely to review my claim.

For a brand that claims to value quality and customer loyalty, this is completely unacceptable. This was a manufacturing defect, not customer damage, yet the burden was entirely placed on me. Delaying a resolution by more than a week shows a clear lack of accountability and care for the customer.

As a result, I will not be making any future purchases from Lululemon. There are many competitors in the market offering similar or better quality—without subjecting their customers to an infuriating and dismissive claims process.

At this rate, with these horrendous policies and zero sense of urgency or responsibility, I genuinely question how long Lululemon will be able to retain loyal customers or remain competitive in this industry. If this is their idea of customer service, I predict they won’t be in business much longer.

When I said that to the agent I was informed you cannot change address unless it is FEDEX. Fedex was not an option for shipping. Then I was told to contact the shipping agent GoBolt. What is the point of the Lululemon customer service then? Why is it my responsibly to contact the shipper. I didn't ship it. The woman Amanda was not helpful in anyway. For my troubles, they said they would send me a 20 dollar gift card. My troubles is that I want the purse I ordered, I want Lululemon customer service to do their jobs instead I had to spend my 30 min lunch making sure GoBolt will reattempt a delivery. title: Long-time Lululemon Customer No More!!!
text:
I’ve been a long-time Lululemon customer, but after my recent experience, I will be rethinking any future purchases. Not only have I been disappointed with the declining quality of their products, but the customer service process has been frustrating, dismissive, and unhelpful.

I reached out to Lululemon regarding a defective product - defective in the way that I wore the workout shirt twice and did not wash it and it had multiple holes. Instead of standing by their so-called “quality promise,” they dismissed my concern as “accidental damage.” Save this future line and deem everything as a "manufacturing issue". To make matters worse, they offered me a 25% off promo code as a consolation when I asked for a replacement or refund. While not ideal, I was willing to accept this, however, they failed to clearly state the expiration details in the last email they had sent. By the time I tried to use it, the code had already expired.

When I followed up, I was met with nothing but canned responses and policies. They flat-out refused to reissue the code and spewed what seemed like generic AI-generated emails that did not always reveal true solutions that came close to exemplifying true quality customer service. Considering the amount of time I’ve wasted emailing back and forth, it’s unacceptable that they couldn’t provide a simple solution like reissuing the coupon they had already promised. An easy solution.

This whole experience shows how little Lululemon values loyal customers. Their product quality is slipping, and their customer service team is rigid and unwilling to resolve issues fairly. A company that once prided itself on premium standards now feels more like a fast-fashion brand with premium prices.

Save yourself the headache - there are plenty of other activewear brands that stand behind their products and treat their customers with respect. Unfortunately, Lululemon no longer seems to be one of them.

title: Horrid customer service
text: I have been a Lululemon online customer for years.  Recently for some reason that customer service can't explain I was locked out of my online account.  Every time I would go to check out and online error code would come up.  When I called customer service, they said someone would get back to me in 4 days.  All my credit card info was correct and there was no apparent reason for the lock out.  When customer service finally got back to me, they stated that I needed to send in a copy of my credit card statement for them to unlock my account.  This is absolutely ridiculous as I did not need to show that when I opened my account.  Also no other company ask for such private info.  When I told them I was not comfortable doing this they said they could not unlock my account.  To check out you need a security code on your credit card as you do in most cases, but this was not good enough for them.  This is definitely not an ethical way to do business.  I even tried to order my items online without an account and they still would not let me until I sent in my credit card statement.  I will no longer be doing business with this company.

title: If I could give a zero star I would

They then told me it is a privacy policy to not release information regarding the cards. They are my cards, I have a right to know where they were used! From there, they then stated that they actually cannot look up any transaction with said cards and that I will get no compensation. I truly believe that there was never a balance on these cards; or they re-use the numbers. Since purchased online, they assume zero responsibility. They told me to contact air miles. They state that the cards are like cash and cannot be traced. I do not believe that for a second. Since they have zero transparency with these kind of purchases…. DO NOT BUY gift cards because if you have an issue, you are never getting that money back and they do not care. This was theft, plain and simple.

title: Another Rip off by Lululemon
text: If I could give no stars I would!  I placed an online order for items I was buying for my granddaughter.  The order was over $400! I was finally able to file a claim (you have to wait 24 hours from the delivery date) and even then you supposedly wait 2 or 3 days until they do an investigation and will offer the most "reasonable solution!"  I am out $267 for which I received nothing.  I asked to have this elevated and the customer service rep states that is not possible.  I have only purchased in store previously.  If you order online be aware that you very possibly could be ripped off.  I have read other reviews that pretty much say the same.  Others have lost money too and have had no resolution.  It will be a cold day in hell before I do business with this company again AND their merchandise is outrageously priced.  You would think for the price of their merchandise they would offer exceptional customer service! title: Lululemon delivery via Veho — $400 order “marked as delivered” with black screen, no help from support
text:
I ordered from Lululemon for over $400, trusting the brand to deliver quality — not just in the product, but in the experience.

Lululemon used Veho as the delivery partner. The package was marked as “delivered”, but I never received it. The “proof of delivery” is literally a photo of a black screen — no visible location, no signature, no human confirmation.

I reached out to Lululemon customer support asking for assistance — not even a refund, just to help me get a response from Veho or escalate the issue.
They refused to help, saying the delivery was “confirmed.” But I have video surveillance from my building that proves no one showed up at the time Veho claimed delivery happened.

I’m a single mother raising a baby. $400 is a significant amount for me. Lululemon’s response was dismissive and lacking all empathy — and no effort was made to investigate or protect me as a customer.

Lululemon, you chose Veho. That makes you responsible.
I expect:

A formal investigation or dispute on my behalf,

A refund or store credit,

A review of your delivery partner’s behavior.

Until then, I will continue sharing my story publicly — because this is not acceptable from a brand that charges premium prices.

title: Faulty foot measurement guide—failed to meet up to the hype
text:
I fell prey to all the cool advertising for this company, before checking reviews. BIG mistake. I used their sizing chart for their sliders and measured my son’s foot at 9.5 inches. Their sizing chart recommended an 8.5 in size. They recommended size 13 for me. 

My son is neurodivergent, and didn’t tell me during the first week of wearing them that it rubbed the top of his foot raw and gave him blisters under the arch. 


I wanted to like them. I ordered their blue and green slider. The comfort was ok, but for me my right foot slid forward over the tip. I didn’t wear them. 

I called and emailed my concerns and they arranged the return, which ended up a fiasco! Multiple phone calls and emails and it appears they don’t take notes bc I had to re-explain whats going on several times. It’s been about 3 weeks since all 3 pairs were sent back, which they confirmed they received. I finally received a store credit for one pair yesterday and sent an email asking them to confirm which pair that credit is for. I have not heard back yet.

I don’t want a credit—their recommendations are faulty and their slider caused harm to my son’s foot. I’ve reached out to them and let them know I don’t want anything to do with their company and waiting to hear back.

title: Lulu uses the worst shipping company…
text: The only reason I gave two stars was because the representatives that I have worked with have been absolutely phenomenal. The customer service is always five stars. My issue with Lululemon is their shipping methods with on track. My last two orders have both gone missing. And after waiting the seven days, the items that were ordered were out of stock. This is extremely frustrating to absolutely love their products, but they use the worst shipping company available. On track has the highest amount of package loss. I will not be placing another order. They have lost a longtime customer due to not letting customers select the shipping method.

title: AVOID AVOID AVOID
text:
I bout a pair of Align leggings as I'd heard so much about how buttery soft the material was. I agreed on first wear until almost immediately the material pilled and balled. After a month they are unwearable (infrequent wear and washing as instructed)
The level of balling is ridiculous. I put in a complaint and they completely fobbed me off saying it wasn't a quality issue. I can tell you - IT IS A QUALITY ISSUE. back and forward with them 3 or 4 times and they are refusing to let me return them. This company is a disgrace and I will NEVER order from them again and I really hope others save their money and not buy their unwearable products.

title: Took my money and didn't send me the product.
text: For such a well renowned company. I actually can't get over what they have done. I ordered shorts online and they were sent to the wrong COUNTRY which was all their fault. Then, they said they would refund me. Fast forward to today and my account was never refunded. I have proof of the order and because they has changed their system and have no proof on their end anymore. There is 'nothing they can do.'

title: BAD QUALITY PRODUCT NEVER AGAIN
text: BAD QUALITY PRODUCT NEVER AGAIN. One wash following "care" instructions = Completely destroyed hoodie. Customer service blamed me, refused to compensate.  It was my first visit, I spend $500 and every single item was horrendous quality, and customer service did nothing about it. I did a chargeback with my CC and won, and I won't be returning nor would I recommend you do.  Absolute scammers, I can't say it enough.  I've had $15 hoodies off Amazon that are far more durable and comfortable. If you do buy this brand (please don't) be prepared to GENTLY HAND WASH WITHOUT SOAP for the lifetime of ownership of any product. Yes, no washing machine, no dryer, or you will absolutely destroy the product.

title: Es gibt leider keinen Retourenschein im…
text: Es gibt leider keinen Retourenschein im Paket, die Größen passen oft auch nicht. Normalerweise trage ich S oder M und bei Lululemon ist bei den Sport Bras sogar L fast zu klein. Leider hat sich die Qualität sehr verschlechtert. Ich habe 2006 einmal eine kurze Hose gekauft, die ist heute noch einwandfrei. Die Sachen, die man jetzt bestellt, sind entweder kratzig oder nur mehr aus Plastik. Bei einer Legging sind gleich die Nahten aufgegangen. Leider nicht mehr zu empfehlen.

title: Poor customer service
text: Poor customer service. Received pants with a permanent crease in them near the shin of the calf. When I contacted customer service for them to uphold their one year satisfaction guarantee, I was told no. They basically said that I created the crease and it did not happen at their manufacturing plant. So the company does not even uphold their own standards. I won’t be shopping with lululemon again.

title: I ordered some shorts on their website…
text: I ordered some shorts on their website and received them with the security tag still attached! I am a busy mom and I do not have time to drive around go to the store to get a tag taken off that shouldn't even have been there from the get go. I'm very disappointed in their lack of customer service as well. When i reached out they seemed indifferent and suggested returning them..... I have no time to go to the post office either ! Please check your items for security tags before shipping them out that is very irresponsible!!!

title: Disappointed in the Quality and Customer Service
text:
I bought the Everywhere Backpack (Black) from Lululemon in April 2024 and within two months the top strap started fraying. By August, it was already partially detached. I only carried a MacBook Air and a few daily items to and from my office—nothing heavy or rough.

I continued using the bag carefully, hoping it wouldn’t get worse. I stopped using a bag a bit because I was not working. Then in April 2025, the strap completely tore off, making the bag unusable.

I contacted Lululemon with photos clearly showing the issue started just two months after purchase. Their response? They claimed it was “wear and tear” and that I was outside the return window—despite photo evidence with dates and a clear timeline showing the damage began well within the one-year Quality Promise. It feels like they’re using the policy as a shield rather than standing by it in good faith. The evidence was solid, and they just didn’t want to take responsibility.

For a company that positions itself as a premium brand, this was incredibly disappointing. The backpack cost nearly $80 and didn’t even last a few months under light use. I expected better quality—and definitely better customer support.

Buyers beware: not everything Lululemon makes is built to last, and they may not stand behind their products even when they clearly fail early.

title: AVOID. Scam dodgy company

When they finally refunded me it was through a link via live chat. Finally received my refund but it was in USD not GBP like I had paid in, so I ended up receiving LESS than I had originally paid. Absolute scammers. Still trying to get my full refund. AVOID this dodgy company at all costs!!!
